# Bee Bomb Honey

> Pure, raw mountain wildflower honey from the Yampa Valley. Our bees are the bomb.

Bee Bomb Honey is a small-batch honey producer based in Steamboat Springs, Colorado. We practice natural, sustainable beekeeping with no chemicals and no shortcuts — just happy bees turning Colorado wildflowers into liquid gold.

**Website:** https://bee-bomb-website.vercel.app
**Facebook:** https://www.facebook.com/people/Bee-Bomb-Honey/61552058123683/

## Contact

- **Address:** 2130 Resort Drive, Unit E, Steamboat Springs, CO
- **Phone:** (303) 641-1659
- **Email:** beebombhoneyco@gmail.com

## Location

Our hives are located throughout the Yampa Valley near Steamboat Springs, Colorado, at elevations between 6,000 and 8,000 feet. Bees forage on high-altitude wildflowers including clover, lupine, and fireweed.

## Products

### Mountain Wildflower Honey
Pure, raw honey harvested from wildflower meadows in the Yampa Valley. Rich, complex flavor with floral notes unique to the Colorado Rockies.
- Tag: Bestseller

### Raw Honeycomb
Straight from the hive — chewy, waxy, and bursting with unfiltered honey. Perfect on a cheese board or eaten by the spoonful.
- Tag: Fan Favorite

### Wildflower Honey Butter
Our mountain wildflower honey whipped with creamy butter. Spread it on warm bread, pancakes, or biscuits for a taste of the Rockies.
- Tag: New

### Berry Honey Jam
Local berries blended with our raw honey — no refined sugar. A sweet, tangy spread that captures Colorado summer in every jar.
- Tag: Seasonal

## How to Buy

Find us at local farmers markets in Steamboat Springs, or visit us at 2130 Resort Drive, Unit E. You can also call (303) 641-1659 or email beebombhoneyco@gmail.com to place an order. Wholesale and bulk orders available — email for a custom quote.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is your honey raw and unfiltered?**
Yes. All honey is 100% raw and unfiltered. We never heat-treat or ultra-filter our honey, so it retains all of its natural enzymes, pollen, and nutrients straight from the hive.

**Where are your bees located?**
Our hives are located throughout the Yampa Valley near Steamboat Springs, Colorado. Bees forage on wildflowers in mountain meadows at elevations between 6,000 and 8,000 feet.

**What makes mountain wildflower honey different?**
Mountain wildflower honey comes from bees that forage on a diverse mix of high-altitude wildflowers. This creates a complex, rich flavor profile that changes subtly with each season, unlike single-source honeys.

**Do you offer wholesale or bulk orders?**
Yes. Whether you're a restaurant, retailer, or planning an event, reach out at beebombhoneyco@gmail.com for a custom quote.

**How should I store honey?**
Store at room temperature in a dry place. Raw honey never expires. If it crystallizes — that's natural — place the jar in warm water to re-liquify it.
